sourceMovie.title: Keoma
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Keoma for the same reason it broke through in 1976: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Enzo G. Castellari keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The western and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: They Call Me Trinity
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Keoma clicked for you, They Call Me Trinity (1970) is a strong next stop: both trade in western and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Keoma?
faq.answer: Try They Call Me Trinity, Young Guns, Django—each shares DNA with Keoma while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Keoma?
faq.answer: Keoma (1976) is best described as Western, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Keoma worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this western lane.
